MOTION For Order Appointing A General Judicial Referee; Mpa
Off Calendar Sep-07-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael T. McKeeman, Esq. of Seyfarth Shaw LLP, Tel. 415.544.1038, for defendant and moving party;
Alex Baghdassarian, Esq. of Peckar & Abramson, PC, Tel. 310.228.1075, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANTS 250 FOURTH DEVELOPMENT LP, GANENDRA "JAY" SINGH, GMS DEVELOPMENT, INC., PARADIGM HOTELS GROUP, LLC and MINT DEVELOPMENT, L.P.'s MOTION For Order Appointing A General Judicial Referee as follows: Defendants' motion for an order appointing a general judicial referee is granted. Per paragraph 15.4.1 of the parties' agreement, all of the claims alleged by plaintiff The Whiting-Turner Contracting Company, including its declaratory relief claim, are to be determined by a general judicial reference procedure pursuant to CCP 638 et seq. Even if the contract language was not clear on this point, which it is, applicable law requires that doubts be resolved in favor of judicial reference. Nor has Whiting-Turner shown that defendants, or any of them, waived their right to judicial reference. Nothing in the parties' correspondence or dealings with each other prior to the filing of this lawsuit shows that defendants intentionally relinquished their right to judicial reference or that they acted inconsistently with maintaining that right and to the prejudice of Whiting-Turner. Order signed. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Nicole Rodich, CSR#11604, nrodich@sftc.org.
